card.intuition: A suite of different technologies (Custom Elements, Shadow DOM, HTML Templates) that allow you to create reusable custom HTML tags that work natively in all browsers without a framework.
card.analogy: Building your own Lego blocks: instead of just using standard 2x4 rectangles (divs and spans), you build a specialized 'Pirate Ship Mast' block (a <user-profile> tag) that you can use anywhere and it always looks and acts the same.
when_to_use: Building a design system that needs to be shared across many different teams and frameworks (React, Vue, Angular)
Creating standalone widgets (like a weather widget) for embedding on 3rd party sites
Future-proofing your UI code to work without external library dependencies
gotchas: Custom element names MUST contain a hyphen (e.g., <my-tag>) to distinguish them from standard tags
Web Components don't have built-in state management like React; you have to handle attributes and properties manually

exercise.prompt: Identify the three pillars of Web Components.
exercise.starter_code:
pillars = [
    "1. ______ Elements (Defining <my-tag>)",
    "2. Shadow ______ (Encapsulation)",
    "3. HTML Templates (Reusing markup)"
]
hints: They aren't standard elements, they are 'Custom'
exercise.test_code:
assert "Custom" in pillars[0]
assert "DOM" in pillars[1]
print("PASS")
